DESCRIPTION:
Float coercion would corrupt prices like 0.1 + 0.2; ensure the
    parser never drops into float at any stage.
NAME: test_parse_price_returns_decimal_not_float

DESCRIPTION:
Bare numerics without ``$`` are rejected — the parser refuses
    to guess on ambiguous strings like ``"Time left: 3d 4h"`` even
    though they contain digits.
NAME: test_parse_price_requires_dollar_anchor

DESCRIPTION:
When no ``$`` is present, the parser falls back to the first bare
    numeric token. (Defensive — ResultCard prices always carry ``$``.)
NAME: test_parse_price_falls_back_to_bare_number_without_dollar

DESCRIPTION:
``Subtotal (3 items) $50.00`` would parse as 3 if the regex took
    the first number. The $-anchored pattern must win.
NAME: test_parse_price_dollar_anchor_beats_bare_integers
